entry.title: Notification creates into QMEL-PHASE field is not update
entry.content:
Hello,

We have recently transitioned to S/4 HANA and encountered an issue with the QMEL-PHASE field in notifications.

In the previous ECC system, this field was automatically updated :

1 Outstanding
2 Postponed
3 In Process
4 Completed
5 Deletion Flag

But in S/4 HANA, this field stay empty.

Have you the same problem, do i miss a new configuration?

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Re: MDG Cloud Ready Mode: Custom Workflow template in SAP build process automation with S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Anirban,

Thanks again for this insightful blog! 

To add to the question from @MustafaBensan , how do you consume the My Inbox app currently? As far as I understood, SAP Workzone can be used for the consumption of Fiori apps, but also as a conduit for connecting diverse SAP and non-SAP applications. I could imagine that this brings the two worlds together of creating / developing custom workflows in BPA and integrating them through consuming all related MDG Cloud Ready apps through SAP Workzone.

Eager to know what you thoughts are on this!

BR, Frederik

entry.title: Re: How to create cost center custom fields using bapi
entry.content: What means "create customer fields using BAPI"? Which BAPI are you using? What is your custom code to call the BAPI? In which table did you add these custom fields?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Product Compliance - Manage Substance - Additional Identifiers
entry.content:
Hi guys,
in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Product Compliance in app "Manage Substance Compliance" there is the section additional identifiers.

As this is a demo system, I want to change the available identifers, but I do not find area to change them - neither in IMG nor in an app.

Searching the internet and SAP, the onyl hint I find is in "what's new in SAP S/4HANA 2023", here the information is given:
----------------
Before you can assign an additional identifier type, you must first create it in Customizing at SAP
Customizing Implementation Guide > Environment, Health and Safety > Foundation for
EHS > General Configuration > Specify Additional Company Substance Identifiers.
--------------
But this is not available in IMG 2408...

Any suggestions are appreciated.


Thanks
Harry

entry.title: E-Invoicing Exchange Summit - Miami
entry.content:
Are you interested in the topic of electronic invoicing? Are you already facing the problem of supporting and processing the different e-invoicing standards?

Join the E-Invoicing Exchange Summit in Maimi on March 24-26, 2025 filled with a wide variety of presentations, user case studies, and panel discussions on digitalized invoicing processes.

To assist clients with e-invoicing, SAP has developed SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. This solution enables the creation, processing, and monitoring of transactional documents and periodic regulatory reports. Learn more about the e-invoicing initiatives for the US here.

Speaker: Martin Lin, Global Digital Compliance Customer Engagement Lead, SAP.

Find more information the summit here. 
REGISTRATION LINK

If you want to connect with SAP users and experts to exchange on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance topics, join the regional collaboration groups. 

Check out the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community.

entry.title: SAC - Updata data loaded from local file
entry.content:
Hello, 

with  a job I added in my SAC model,  data from local file ( like a budget) , and protect the data loaded again transactional  data from SAP .

like 

DATE, CUSTOMER ID ,NAME , FUNCTION PARTNER, QTY, VALUE

Now I need to update Function partner based on what I have in transactional data because si changed in SAP.

I don't want to create a new job, map and transform data, validate etc.

If there a way to update these data with  ( stupid example) update ..from ..where ? 

Kind regards

Gildo

entry.title: Re: Anyone who have encountered the configuration of CRT...
entry.content:
In Canada, all Earnings and Deductions Wage Types should cumulate according to the Fiscal Year (Y) and based on when the payment/deduction is made (K), on the basis of January to December, and this is usually automatically done when yougenerate the cumulation periods for the next year.  So all cumulators should have been generated for 2025, and you'll have to generate the cumulators for 2026 next november or december at the latest.

But if you have custom cumulators that start mid-year (July-June or April-March), those are probably associated to the year they end in, so the cumulator for April-March must be created for 2026 if it is to be used in April 2025.  Please check V_T56C3 to confirm that you have done so.

entry.title: Re: How to create cost center custom fields using bapi
entry.content:
Hi Sandra_Rossi

My requirement is i have cost center custom fields using standard bapi i have to update csks tables I see there is parameters extensionsin to fill cost center custom fields.but no luck.could you help to fill cost center Custom fields using BAPI

entry.title: Re: File Handling In RAP ( Unmanaged )
entry.content: RAP doesn't mean "Rapid Application Programming" but "RESTful Application Programming"

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Copy objects between Spaces within the s...
entry.content:
Hey Hiri, 

very same way as with transporting to different tenant. Just during import, you do space-mapping (avl from Q3/2024 I guess). Or you just go the old-fashioned way and do the export-replace-import json file. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/b607a12931d74c4a93506ea64c55ab4e.html

 

Cheers,

Martin

entry.title: XSUAA BTP Service Implementation for Custom Python APP
entry.content:
Introduction


In the realm of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), XSUAA (Extended Services for User Authentication and Authorization) plays a crucial role in managing secure access to applications. Integrating XSUAA with a custom Python Flask app can significantly enhance the security by leveraging OAuth2 and JWT tokens for authentication and authorization. This blog post will guide you through the process of setting up and integrating XSUAA with a Python Flask application.

Pre-requisites

Before diving into the implementation, ensure you have the following:

SAP BTP Account: Access to an SAP BTP account with necessary entitlements.
Python: Installed on your local machine (preferably Python 3.6+). or we can use BAS as well

Step 1: Configuring XSUAA
Set Up XSUAA Service in SAP BTP:

Log in to your SAP BTP Cockpit.
Navigate to your subaccount and create an instance of the XSUAA service.

Subscribe the service and generate the service key which will be used later for connecting to this service

Service key looks like this.

It consists of client secret id and password.

Once this steps are done we can proceed with python implementation 

lets create a flask based Flask python application which is hosted on cloud foundry of BTP

To host a python file over the BTP we need below files mandatory.

1. Main Python file(logic)

2. Requirements.txt file

3. Manifest.yml file

4. Runtime.txt file

File structure will look like this 

Lets quickly create all this file for a basic implementation.

One point to Note here: To test XSUAA Service we need to push file to the CF, we cannot test it locally.

So we will create 3 end points with flask in python

Herer we are importing all the libraries that are needed for this implementation.

name will be the XSUAA BTP service name.

Since this doesn't works in local environment we will push this to CF after all routes are done.

APPEnv is the cloud foundry environment once app is deployed over CF so from the env we are using the XSUAA service that we created in the previous section.

1. To generate the Token

This end point will help to get a Auth token 

2. Validate the Generated Token

This end point help us to validate any token coming from external source

3. How to make public url private with this bearer generated token.

This is main Logic end point. generally for development we don't create end points to generate and validate.

Just for testing we have created here.

As you can see if i dont keep a validation over the /testroute it will be a public url but now i have defined a method to check validity of the token. So it will first validate than only my actual logic will give result.

Overall Python code looks like

once main logic is done we will create 3 other required files.

Manifest.yml

Manifest file contains all the necessary information needed for the deployment of the python app.

name : The name of the python application

Memory and disk quotes: This are the space your application going to occupy over the CF

Command: The main file which is gonna execute once your app is deployed.

services: Here we gonna link the XSUAA service name which is there on the BTP

Requirements.txt

List of all the libraries needed for the application.

Runtime.txt

 

This consists of the runtime python environment version which is going to be used by CF.

Once all this files are setup. You can push them to the CF.

For this I am using BAS. SO i just need to login into my CF and go to the root folder of the python app.

Once I am in the root folder i can just deploy by using CF Push command in the command terminal.

Once the app is deployed successfully you will get the complete URL in the command terminal or else also you can get from the BTP as well.

 

 

You will get the above logs once python app is successfully deployed.

Now lets head to chrome to do some basic testing.

The app is working as expected.

Now you can use this token and send it to the /validate root or /testroute to test with postman.

 

Just use with bearer auth the generated token and call your python routes.

Hence we successfully converted public Api calls to private with BTP XSUAA service.

entry.title: Re: Link between Stories
entry.content:
Dear @Ganjos,

you can add hyperlinks to texts/text boxes to access other stories and even specify a dedicated page if needed.
Like in the example below:

For further details, please have a look at the SAP Help Portal
Linking to an External URL or to Another Page or Story (Optimized) 
Select a Story for the Hyperlink 

I hope this helps.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Please advise how to write on WBS Budget Availability Control tables (BPJA e BPGE) from SAC
entry.content:
Please advise on which is the Standard API available to recall from SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning to write the budget on the budget availability control tables for the WBS (BPJA and BPGE) on S/4HANA RISE Private Cloud.

If Standard API is not available which of the below solution is advisable?

1. Write first from SAC to ACDOCP leveraging SAC standard export to S/4HANA through SAC Data Management and then move the data to BPJA e BPGE in S/4 

2. Recall a custom API from SAC and leverage an existing FM (RFC KBPP_EXTERN_UPDATE_CO) to write on BPJA e BPGE in S/4.

Thank you!

Giorgio

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh (Integration Suite) service binding not po...
entry.content: For future readers: Be careful, don't activate the Event Mesh Subscription (Standard plan) if you use the IS embedded EM (EMIS). The mentioned one is the stand alone EM and it can't coexist with EMIS on the same tenant, afaik.

entry.title: Re: Rf scanning QR codes in warehouse processes

We helped you to accept the answer since there were no further updates under the topic.

You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions. 

 

Best regards,

Geovana

entry.content:

Best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Purchase order Release strategy's status is wrong in ME23N
entry.content:
The purchase order workflow is completed, and all the approvers are approved. The PO has been released but is still in ME23N, and the status of the Release strategy is pending in the header details. 
In the workflow log, there is no error, and all the tasks are executed perfectly, what is the root cause for this behavior in the s4 Hana system, and how to resolve this?

entry.title: Re: Learning License User Types in SAP SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content: Thanks for pointing that out! Functional users set up would be different in IAS /IPS.

entry.title: Re: What is a sales area ?
entry.content:
Sales Area - combination 

Sales Org + Distribution Channel + Division

Sales Assignment  SPRO > Assignment > Sales Area

entry.content:

the standard orders are not invoiced to me.

Do you know what this could be due to and how it can massively bill standard orders through some job?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Ismael.

entry.title: about vulnerability CVE-2025-0066 in SAP note 3550708
entry.content:
hello everyone,
I am new to the community, I need help
we have sap basis 731 is in sp level 34, according to the note it should be upgraded to sp level 36 and that upgrade would disable the SA38 or remove the permission for SA38 in current sp level 34, is that correct?
has anyone of you already applied the correction? what is the option regarding the use of SA38 for sap administrators?

Solution
The correction disables the program execution of SA38 transaction.
Please implement the support package referenced in this SAP Security Note, or implement the respective correction instruction.
If you have further questions, refer to SAP Note 3550790.
Workaround
Please assess the workaround applicability for your SAP landscape prior to implementation.
Note that this workaround is a temporary fix and is not a permanent solution. SAP strongly recommends you apply the corrections outlined in the security note, which can be done in lieu of the workaround or after the workaround is implemented.
Do not grant permission to execute transaction SA38.

entry.title: Creation of WBS using CNMASSCREATE
entry.content:
Hi SAP Gurus,

How can I create a Level 3 WBS element in an existing project in SAP PS under a Level 2 WBS element? How does the system ensure that the newly created WBS element is correctly placed under the Level 2 WBS element in sequential order? When I download the Template from CNMASSCREATE there are some fields are not available like Project definition, WBS name, description. How can I get those details in the template and when I upload the template in the CNMASSCREATE I got an error that WRONG FILE UPLOADED.  Can you please help me? @SAPPS PLM Project System (PS)

entry.title: Re: SAC - Updata data loaded from local file
entry.content:
Hi @egil63,

 

 

In SAP Analytics Cloud you can create an upload job in the model. Once the upload job is set up, there's no need to map it repeatedly. Simply maintain the Excel file according to the upload job requirements.

In the story, you can upload the Excel file directly and publish the data.

If you find this helpful, kindly upvote!

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP PPDS Tank Planning Batch production
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a requirement for a project of ePPDS, I'm in a batch production mode, and need to use tank planning for the following scenario:

Operation 1: Production of a Semi Finished (Cream) on a production resource (Resource 1), this activity is occuring in parallel with the filling of a Tank with the produced quantity

Operation 2: Rest Time in the Tank for the cream

Operation 3: Draining of Tank

To model this in ePPDS I think I should put in the routing the activity of production (on Resource 1) to be in parallel with Filling activity (on Tank Resource) and also the Operation 2 and 3 in the same routing of the semi finished.

1st question : The question is which type of control key I need to use for each filling and draining activities on the Tank ?  

2nd question : I did also the needed custo in SPRO to enable the tank planning, but is there any other tasks to do especially to correctly generate the 'Product Storage Definition' via the PDS ?

 

PS : The Tank resource is a storage resource (CUV02)

Any help or hints are highly appreciated! 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: I_Product not defind error in eclipse when creating n...
entry.content: you need s4 system, not an empty abap box

entry.title: Customer returns to be posted directly to Quality without creating an inspection lot
entry.content:
I need customer returns to be posted directly to Quality without creating an inspection lot, which can be achieved without activating the inspection setup.

As well as, Quality Inspection lots are created for Inspection Type 04 (Goods Receipt Inspection from Production).

While both processes can be managed separately, activating Inspection Type 04 prevents customer returns from being posted to Quality without an inspection lot.

I read through most threads but couldn't find an answer.

#QM PLM Quality Management (QM)

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
@etardiff 

RE:

Hello,

The new F90 entry did not work as expected. When I split the entry between F38 delimited 2024-06-24 and F90 effective from 2024-06-25,

the wage type was not reported at all. However, when I put the wage type in both F38 and F90 for the entire year, it reported the full amount in both.

Can someone shed some light on how to get this to work correctly?

We used a BAdI to split them.

entry.title: Re: How to Add Check Box in RAP APPLICATION
entry.content:
Hi @agrawalamit20 

Two things needs to be checked 

1- data type of the status field should be Boolean type.

2- You need to have default delete method or action in behavior definition.

if no action then framework render Boolean field as radio button which is single select.

observed these with V4 api…

Hope this helps…

Thanks-

I go to my learnings > subscription, but there is no subscription. I don't know how to activate the subscription. Can someone help guide me through the process?

entry.title: "Edit" button not available in FIORI App "Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting-Specific"
entry.content: Edit button is disabled in "Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting" app

entry.title: Re: Datasphere THE solution to maintain the business context & considerations for outbound data
entry.content:
Hi @nachtaktiv,

Thanks for reading my blog!

Currency conversions, unit of measures, hierarchies, etc. and their relationships with data are key to giving business meaning to the data. From a business data perspective, without them, there would be no business understanding on the on the data at all. That's why SAP refers to them as Business Context. These elements are part of the semantic layer of S/4HANA, and the purpose of my blog is for our great community to better understand how to maintain them with SAP Datasphere for enterprise data and analytics purposes vs potentially losing them when moving SAP data out.

Similar to the pursuit of the one domain model, CDS views serve as the vehicle to deliver the data model for the enterprise. Since S/4HANA manages data across multiple business functions, these views need to be combined and modeled for enterprise analytics purposes. That's what they are for. Looking ahead, SAP remains committed to strengthen and provide more content and attributes to data products to be even more relevant in the future.

I hope this explanation provides clarity!

entry.title: Re: about vulnerability CVE-2025-0066 in SAP note 3550708
entry.content:
The note 3550790 - FAQ for SAP Security Note 3550708 - Information Disclosure vulnerability in SAP NetWeaver AS for ABAP and ABAP Platform - SAP for Me is only a Q&A about the note 3550708. Nowhere is said in the note 3550790 that SA38 is disabled. The most important information is that it explains why the security note 3550708 was disclosed: "Using report RSICFCLTST01 credentials can be retrieved".

Now, what contains the referred note 3550708 - [CVE-2025-0066] Information Disclosure vulnerability in SAP NetWeaver AS for ABAP and ABAP Platform(Internet Communication Framework) - SAP for Me?

  • It adds the statement "RETURN" at the beginning of the report RSICFCLTST01, which means that if you run the program, nothing happens, credentials cannot be retrieved (by hackers) anymore.

So, no worry about SA38, it's still there.

entry.title: Wildcard Character for WEBi Search
entry.content: Hello Community, I am trying to find a wildcard character that will return all inventory numbers beginning with the prefix 'AB' (for example, AB12345, AB12346, AB12347, etc.) I have set my operator to both 'Matches Pattern' and 'In LIst' and typed the input as both 'AB*', and 'AB%' but it does not return any results. Any help on this is greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Freecash Bonus Code {FC2002}: Earn $250 Free Sign Up & Referral Bonus
entry.content:
Use the latest Freecash bonus code, FC2002, to get free coins, which you can exchange for gift cards and crypto rewards. Keep in mind, though, this bonus code is only applicable if you’ve reached level 10 or higher. So, level up to take full advantage of it!

New users can claim up to $250 in bonuses by signing up with the referral code FC2002. On top of that, you can join Freecash’s affiliate program and earn a minimum 5% commission on referrals.

In this article, you’ll find all the updated Freecash bonus codes along with step-by-step instructions on how to claim them.

Freecash Bonus Code Today 2025: Use (FC2002) For Reward

Find all the latest Freecash bonus codes to earn extra coins and rewards.

Freecash bonus code 2025 – FC2002

Freecash bonus code today – FC2002

Freecash bonus code – FC2002

Freecash bonus code today – FC2002

Freecash bonus code February – FC2002

Freecash bonus code January – FC2002

Use these codes to boost your earnings and get the most out of Freecash!

How To Apply Freecash Bonus Code?

Download the Freecash app and visit FreeCash reward page.

Click on the button “Bonus Code” to enter the code.

Reach level 10 or above to apply code.

Enter the latest FreeCash bonus code (FC2002) and apply.

You will receive free skins and coins.

What is Freecash Bonus Code?

Freecash runs promotions where they drop bonus codes on their social media platform such as Twitter and Instagram for users to earn extra FreeCash coins and mystery boxes. In order to get these codes you need to be active on their social media platforms.

The Freecash bonus code is (FC2002) that will give up to $250 welcome bonus. User must use this code before signing up.

FreeCash also runs many promotions on its platform like a daily ladder bonus (req. lev.1) and an affiliate program to earn daily free bonuses.

Freecash Affiliate Program: Earn Maximum 30% Commission On Referrals Earning

Freecash launched a new referral program where users can earn 30% of their referral earnings along with referral bonus. For every new referral, FreeCash offers 10-100 coins (depending on user tasks).

How To Earn Freecash Referral Bonus?

Open FreeCash app or login into your browser.

Click on “Menu> affiliates” in left top corner.

Copy your Freecash referral code or link from the tab.

Share your referral link with your friends and family member.

Once your friends join via your link you both will earn FreeCash bonus.

Is Freecash legit?

Yes! FreeCash is completely legit and has millions of users on its platform who are earning money in their free time. As they mentioned “An average user on FreeCash earn a minimum of $10.73 through task and surveys. Till now FreeCash paid more than $2.9 million to its users.

Freecash have more than 4.5 stars on Trustpilot and there customer reviews are also very good. Users have been making a decent amount of money through Various Freecash task and Freecash bonus codes are ice on the cake.

Conclusion

FreeCash is a great platform to earn money in your free time. With Freecash users can take surveys, play games, watch videos and get paid instantly. The bonus code of freecash app will double your earning and offer you exciting prizes.

FAQs

What is the Freecash app bonus code 2024?

The latest Freecash app bonus code 2024 is “FC2002”. Use it while signing up and claim up to a $250 sign up bonus.

How To Reach Level 1 on Freecash?

To reach level 1 or above on Freecash, users need to earn 1000 coins through survey and tasks. Go to Freecash task page and find surveys and tasks to complete. The more coin you earn the highest level you achieve on Freecash.

FAQs on Freecash Bonus Code

1. What is a Freecash Bonus Code?

A Freecash bonus code is a special promotional code that users can apply on the Freecash platform to earn extra rewards, such as free coins, gift cards, or crypto. These codes can be used to boost earnings when completing tasks like watching videos or taking surveys.

2. How do I use a Freecash bonus code?

To use a Freecash bonus code:

Sign up or log in to your Freecash account.

Go to the "Redeem" section on the platform.

Enter the bonus code (e.g., FC2002) and click apply.

You will receive rewards based on the code, but some codes may require you to reach a certain level (like level 10) before applying.

3. What rewards can I earn with Freecash bonus codes?

Using a Freecash bonus code can give you:

Free coins

Gift cards for major retailers

Cryptocurrency rewards (such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.) The amount varies based on the code and any special offers at the time.

4. How often does Freecash release new bonus codes?

Freecash regularly releases new bonus codes, especially during promotions, holidays, or special events. Check their social media pages, newsletters, or the platform itself to stay updated on the latest codes.

5. Can I use a Freecash bonus code more than once?

Most Freecash bonus codes are single-use, meaning you can only apply them once per account. However, Freecash releases new codes regularly, so you can continue to benefit from future promotions.

6. What should I do if my Freecash bonus code isn’t working?

If a bonus code isn’t working, it could be due to:

Expiration: The code may have expired.

Level restrictions: Some codes require you to reach a certain user level (e.g., level 10).

Incorrect code entry: Double-check the code for any typos. If issues persist, contact Freecash’s customer support for assistance.

7. Can I earn extra rewards by referring others to Freecash?

Yes, Freecash offers a referral program. When you invite others to join Freecash using your unique referral link, both you and the referred user can earn rewards, including a bonus when they sign up and complete tasks.

8. What is the current Freecash bonus code for 2024?

The latest Freecash bonus code for 2024 is FC2002. Use it to earn rewards such as free coins and crypto when you sign up or level up on the platform.

9. Are there any restrictions when using a Freecash bonus code?

Some Freecash bonus codes come with restrictions, such as:

Minimum user level (e.g., level 10) before using the code.

Single-use per account.

Expiration dates, meaning codes must be used before they expire.

10. How can I stay updated on the latest Freecash bonus codes?

You can stay updated on the latest Freecash bonus codes by:

Following Freecash on social media platforms.

Subscribing to their email newsletter.

Checking their website for any ongoing promotions or offers.

entry.title: Re: No data being read from SAP Gateway Demo Server - ES5...
entry.content: don't u see that 401 error?

entry.title: SAP HANA Database Server Migration on GCP Bare Metal Instance
entry.content:
SAP HANA Database Server Migration on  GCP: From m2-megamem-416 to x4-megamem-960-metal(bare metal)

This blog post outlines key considerations and steps for migrating your SAP HANA database server from Google Cloud Platform's (GCP) m2-megamem-416 machine type to the more powerful x4-megamem-960-metal.

Requirement of Migration:

  • Performance Boost: x4-megamem-960-metal offers significantly higher CPU cores and memory capacity, leading to substantial performance improvements for demanding SAP HANA workloads.
  • Enhanced Scalability: The increased resources provide greater headroom for future growth and accommodate expanding data volumes and user demands.
  • Cost Optimization: While initially more expensive, the x4-megamem-960-metal can potentially lead to cost savings in the long run due to improved performance and resource utilization.
  • Latest Technology: Migrating to the x4-megamem-960-metal allows you to leverage the latest Intel Xeon Scalable processors and optimized hardware for SAP HANA.
  • My scenario : In our case HANA Database was using 70-80% available CPU on m2-megamem-416 with AARE config and there is no next machine type available to support high cpu demand on same family 

Migration Planning & Execution:

  • Assessment:

    • Workload Analysis: Analyze your current SAP HANA workload on m2-megamem-416 to understand resource utilization patterns Specically memory utilization and future growth of HANA Database,CPU bottleneck , performance bottlenecks, and future growth projections
    • Sizing: Determine the optimal x4-megamem-960-metal instance size based on your workload analysis and future requirements. Consider factors like CPU, memory, and storage needs.(Do sizing exercise before making decision on server size)
    • Backup & Recovery: Ensure you have a robust backup and recovery strategy in place before initiating the migration.
  • Preparation:

    • System Check: Perform thorough system checks on both the source (m2-megamem-416) and target (x4-megamem-960-metal) instances.
    • Check and validate HANA Supported version,Supported Disk type ,Supported OS version.
    • Perform check SAP HANA Hardware and Cloud Measurement Tools on new server and go through all the listed KPI 

            M2 Machine Type (m2-megamem-416😞 

 

    M2 machine type storage limits

 

 

X4 machine types  (x4-megamem-960-metal)

 

 

 

  • Software Updates: Update SAP HANA and related software components on both instances to the latest stable versions.

Migration Methods:

  • SAP HANA system replication
  • SAP HANA backup and recovery
  • Machine type change

Comparing these methods

 

  • Post-Migration:

    • Performance Monitoring: Closely monitor the performance of SAP HANA on the x4-megamem-960-metal instance after the migration.
    • Testing: Conduct thorough testing of all SAP HANA functionalities and applications to ensure proper operation.
    • Fine-tuning: Fine-tune SAP HANA parameters and system configurations on the new instance to optimize performance and resource utilization.

Key Considerations:

  • Licensing: Ensure you have the appropriate SAP HANA licenses for the increased resources of the x4-megamem-960-metal instance.
  • Networking: Plan for network connectivity and bandwidth requirements for data transfer and ongoing operations.
  • Storage: Select the appropriate storage solution for the x4-megamem-960-metal instance, considering factors like performance, capacity, and cost.
  • Support: Leverage GCP support resources and SAP support for assistance throughout the migration process.

 

X4 Limitations : 

The X4 machine series is only available as predefined machine types. Custom machine shapes are not available. The following additional restrictions apply:

  1. You can't attach Persistent Disk volumes to an X4 instance, only Hyperdisk volumes.
  2. The X4 machine series is available in only select zones and regions.
  3. X4 instances aren't supported with all operating system images. The operating system details page shows which operating system versions can be used with X4 instances.
  4. If using a custom image, when you create the image, you must enable the UEFI-compatible OS feature.
  5. You can't use GPUs with X4 instances.
  6. Shielded VM isn't supported with bare metal instances.
  7. Live migration is not supported with X4 instances.
  8. There's no hypervisor provided with X4 bare metal instances and nested virtualization isn't enabled.
  9. X4 machine series instances can take up to 30 mins to boot due to large hardware and the Power-On Self-Test (POST).
  10. The interactive serial console is not available for the X4 machine series. For more information, see Troubleshooting using the serial console.

Manage host maintenance events for X4 instances running SAP HANA : 

 

  1. X4 is a specialized series of bare metal Compute Engine machine types specifically designed to handle multi-terabyte SAP HANA workloads. To ensure optimal performance, security, and reliability, X4 instances require regular software and firmware updates. These updates are delivered through planned host maintenance events.

Crucially, unlike other Compute Engine machine types, X4 instances do not support live migration during maintenance. This means that Google must temporarily stop and update your X4 instances, which can have an impact on your SAP HANA workloads

There are two ways maintenance event can be trigger

1.Maintenance event manually triggered by you

 

2. Maintenance event automatically triggered by Google

 




Reference : 

  • SAP HANA Hardware and Cloud Measurement Tools

https://help.sap.com/docs/HANA_HW_CLOUD_TOOLS/02bb1e64c2ae4de7a11369f4e70a6394/7e878f6e16394f2990f126e639386333.html

SAP Note 1943937

  • Host maintenance events

https://cloud.google.com/solutions/sap/docs/manage-maintenance-events-for-bare-metal-instances

  • Migrate SAP HANA to a Compute Engine bare metal instance

https://cloud.google.com/solutions/sap/docs/migrate-hana-to-bare-metal-instance

  • SAP HANA on Bare Metal Solution deployment guide

https://cloud.google.com/solutions/sap/docs/sap-hana-on-bms-deployment

entry.title: How to mass delete physical inventory documents on sap public cloud
entry.content:
There have been more than 1500 physical inventory documents created by mistake. We need a solution to delete them on public cloud.

As of now, could find jus one option which is to delete individually.

Need a solution by which can delete in mass.

entry.title: SoapFault:faultstring=Error reading XMLStreamReader: Unexpected character
entry.content:
Hi Expert

I’m facing an issue while working on a SOAP web service call in my ABAP program, and I’m seeking your guidance.

Error Details:

During the execution of the web service, I encounter the following error:

Scenario:

  • Web Service Type: SOAP
  • Proxy Class: Used for calling the service in ABAP.
  • Payload: Sent from the ABAP program using a generated proxy class.

The error seems to suggest that the response or request payload contains invalid characters or is not properly formatted XML. This happens when sending the SOAP request to a CPI endpoint.

What I’ve Checked So Far:

  1. Payload Structure:

    • Ensured the payload has the correct SOAP envelope and namespace definitions.
    • Verified all required fields are populated.

  2. CPI Logs:

    • Checked logs in SAP Integration Suite (CPI) and observed issues during payload processing.

  3. Proxy Configuration:

    • Verified endpoint, authentication, and proxy configurations in the ABAP program.

  4. Request Validation:

    • Used HTTP tools (Postman , soapUI) to send the same payload, and it worked as expected, pointing to an issue in the ABAP-generated payload.

 

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,
Taher

entry.title: Deprecated domains and Data elements - Pre conversion project
entry.content:
Hi SAP team, 

we need guidance on below issue:

Context:

Ford is doing a S/4HANA conversion for ECC to S/4HANA. We have N and N 1 Landscape in Ford. 

N is for current Business As Usual landscape.

N 1 Landscape is being created for S/4HANA where system copies from N will be copied in N 1 and SUM tool will be executed.

Issue:

Option 1: In N 1 DEV conversion - we will come across a stage in Uptime phase of SUM - ACT_UPG where we will remediate deprecated domains and data elements with custom domains and data elements. These will be captured in a TR in N 1 DEV. This TR will be imported in N landscape and will be tested and moved to Production ( In N Landscape)

Option 2: In N 1 DEV conversion - we will come across a stage in Uptime phase of SUM - ACT_UPG where we will remediate deprecated domains and data elements with custom domains and data elements. These will be captured in a TR in N 1 DEV. This TR will NOT move in N Landscape and will go in N 1 QA, N 1 TEST and finally in PROD.

Option 3: Make changes directly in PROD uptime phase.

Question:

When we will do Upgrade/conversion for Production will we get an option to import the TR that we created in N 1 DEV in Production conversion in Uptime phase ? Will it pose any problems if we go ahead with Option 2 as above ?

what will be best strategy for this considering all above 3 options.

entry.title: Re: Anyone who have encountered the configuration of CRT...
entry.content:
Check V_T549Z for  your Custom Cumulator and Years 2025 and 2026.

For each Modif./Param. in Year 2025, the first X Payroll Periods should be associated with Cumulation Year 2025, while the last Y Payroll Periods should be associated with Cumulation Year 2026.

Check the configuration of the past years (such as 2022 or 2023) to confirm.

If that solves your problem, make a note to modify this table each year during the Payroll Calendars generation process.

entry.title: Mexico payables with two currencies in XK03
entry.content:

What setup does SAP need to know when to pay USD in local Fx currency to a MXN bank account VS paying USD to same currency bank destination?

I am working on the multicurrency for Mexico payables, but need more information on this from a fundational standpoint for SAP configuration and testing before moving into PROD or ECP.

entry.title: MaxDB restore failing
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

one data file of our prod. MaxDB has been zeroed. No idea how could that happen!
Now I am trying to restore, using DB Studio. I selected a point in time of today morning 7 AM.
This failed miserably, here are the errors from the log:

I wanted to attempt another restore to just restore the last Full DB Backup. We made it available in a local file system directly on the server. When I checked now, THE BACKUP FILE WAS GONE !!!! HOW IS THIS POSSIBLE ???

Could anyone please help with some meaningful explanations how can we manage the restore ? Many thanks!

entry.title: Re: Producer Keeps Crashing
entry.content:
Hi Sanchia,

It is possible that Producer is not crashing but that there is a slow running process.  The Watchdog window appears to inform the author that something is not responding so it might be an issue.  You can increase the time before Watchdog reports a potential issue if it is not really an issue but a slow running process.  What happens if you click Please wait?

If you do click Please wait several times and nothing occurs, I would start the investigation by confirming that the author that is having issues has the correct prerequisites to use Producer.  You can do that using the Enable Now Cloud Requirements document.  At a minimum, make sure that the following requirements are in place:

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - SAP Community Escape House - UI5
entry.content: Me patiently waiting until February for the next room. 😭

entry.title: CDS view for Decision Text mentioned in the My Inbox while releasing the Sales Order
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

 

Could you please inform us the CDS view for the string field Decision Text that appears when a Sales Order is being released in the My Inbox app?

 

Best regards,

Customer


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDS view for Decision Text mentioned in the My Inbox...
entry.content:
Hello customer,

 

The CDS Database View is I_WorkflowTaskResultReasonText

The string content that is inside the "Decision Text" field code is WORKFLOWTASKRESULTREASON - WORKFLOWTASKRESULTREASONTEXT

However, this CDS View is still not released by SAP Developers, so you won't be able to use it yet.

 

We kindly suggest that you create a feature request for this business requirement.
This will inform our developers as to what additional functionality should be considered for future releases. You can check the following article for further reference 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product and follow the steps below.

 

1. Get started by opening SAP Customer Influence.

2. Register and accept the Terms of Use.

3. Choose a session of your interest.

4. Create and submit your improvement request.

5. Take a look at existing improvement requests and start voting and commenting.


When you go there, please request for the Release of the CDS View I_WorkflowTaskResultReasonText.

 

SAP Customer Influence enables customers to submit improvement requests and to openly vote on them. These voting results inform SAP of product enhancements and changes customers are most interested in seeing in future releases.


Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Checklist for Service Procurement Integration SAP Fieldglass, Ariba and S/4HANA Solutions
entry.content: Blog to to be posted soon

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud User and Team Provisioning SCIM API Best Practices and Sample Scripts
entry.content:
Hello @Matthew_Shaw,

I'll write again here. First of all, thank you very much for your support

I have analyzed your text multiple times and understood the following. Is this correct?

Authentication: Takes place in Azure AD. In Azure AD, there are currently two authentication methods: either with the email address or the UPN (which is also an email address). ???

Authorization: Takes place in SAC, based on the Subject Name Identifier (IAS). The user in SAC is authorized/found based on the Subject Name Identifier (from IAS). The user attributes in SAC (USER ID, email address, or a custom SAML attribute) must match the Subject Name Identifier. For example Subject Name Identifier (IAS) -> dpeck = USER ID (SAC) -> dpeck. And in SAC, the field USER ID under users must contain "dpeck". ???

This means that when I access a link to my SAC tenant, I can’t enter an Employee ID (e.g., “12344”) or USER ID (e.g., “dpeck”), but only an email address, as authentication takes place in Azure. Did I understand that correctly? However, authorization (Subject Name Identifier) can also take place using the Employee ID (e.g., “12344”). Did I understand that correctly?

Thank you very much

entry.title: Checklist for Service Procurement Integration SAP Fieldglass, Ariba and S/4HANA Solutions
entry.content:
Welcome to the Checklist for Service Procurement Integration SAP Fieldglass, Ariba and S/4HANA Solutions

Target Audience

This blog is for implementors and administrators from customer and partner organizations. It’s a living document that will be updated regularly, so bookmark this page and revisit for the latest insights. Got a question? Drop it in the Q&A section at the bottom of the page!

Why Service Procurement Integration Matters

Did you know that employees now make up only 58% of workforce spend? The other 42% goes to external resources. This data, from Services Procurement Insights: The Big Reveal, underscores the rising importance of managing external services efficiently.

In today’s business landscape, organizations come from diverse backgrounds and unique journeys. This heterogeneity can make service procurement integration challenging. But here’s the good news: streamlining your Purchase Requisition (PR), Purchase Order (PO), Service Entry Sheets (SES), and Invoice processes can give you tighter control over your procurement lifecycle.

Why is this critical? For one, you don’t want to fall victim to scams where fraudulent invoices got paid. Tight integration can help you avoid paying for services without authorized POs and verified proof of delivery. (funny but real story)

The Benefits of Integration

Integrating PR/PO, SES, and Invoice processes isn’t just about compliance; it’s about transformation. Here’s what you stand to gain:

  • Fraud Prevention: Never pay invoices without an authorized PO and proof of service delivery.
  • Enhanced Visibility: Get a complete picture of your service procurement spend.
  • Improved Compliance: Stay audit-ready and meet regulatory standards.
  • Streamlined Performance: Reduce inefficiencies and manual interventions.

Your Options for Integration

Here are three solid solutions for integrating end-to-end service procurement applications:

  1. ERP Add-on by ASAPIO
    • For customers on SAP ECC or S/HANA MM-SRV
  2. 22K Solution
    • For Customers who prefers direct integration between SAP Fieldglass and SAP S/4HANA (lean services)
  3. 4R2 Solution
    • For Customers who like to integrate SAP Fieldgalss and SAP S/4HANA(lean services) via SAP Ariba

Questions to Answer Before You Start

Before diving into implementation, take a moment to reflect on these key questions:

  1. What cost objects will your services spend be allocated to?
    • Think cost centers, internal orders, maintenance orders or projects. Clarifying this early will guide your financial postings.
  2. What version of SAP S/4HANA are you using?
    • Features and compatibility often hinge on your specific SAP S/4HANA version (MM-SRV vs lean services a key decision criteria).  For customers who are adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud, they will automatically start only on lean services however many customers on-prem or private cloud may need to take care of this migration.  MM-SRV to lean services migration can be found here: Link: https://me.sap.com/notes/2906140
  3. Why can integration be complicated?
    • Integration complexity grows exponentially due to:
      • Types of cost objects
      • Business documents (PR, PO, SES, Invoice)
      • Actions like create, revise, or close
  4. What about performance considerations?
    • A major bottleneck can be the number of SES entries per single PO line item. Plan your system design to avoid performance issues.

Let’s Hear From You!

Have questions, thoughts, or tips to share? Drop them in the comments below, and let’s start a conversation!

entry.title: CDS view:get current year from date of one association and use it as condition in other association
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement to write a Query reporting that list out the Purchase requisition Workflow details with the combination of FM data (FMHIENODES and FMBASOBJNR).

I created one CDS view to have all PR details called _PRBase and other one to have all the FM data _FMData. _PRBase will go with item wise of Purchase requisition and the _FMData will go with Fiscal year wise FM data. So the WBS element of one PR item data will be having a multiple data in the FM (the same WBS element value will be stored to multiple Fiscal years).

Here the unique data or field as WBS element field value from _PRBase and the Measure field value from _FMData. This _FMData has multiple measure data based on Fiscal year. Therefore to avoid the duplicates , I have to add one more condition to filter the Year used in the creationdate of _PRBase with the Fiscal Year used in the _FMData.

How to achieve this in the calling association of the above two views? 

Please answer with the possible solutions to achieve this requirement.

Thanks.

entry.title: Release announcement of SAP Emarsys Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hello!

We are pleased to inform you about the release of our SAP Emarsys Stay Certified Assessment.

The SAP Emarsys Stay Certified, provides you the opportunity to familiarize yourself with the latest updates and product changes and keep your certification valid. The Stay Certified program contains 

  • SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement Stay Certified Learning - This course will help you learn about the recent enhancements in SAP Emarsys  to prepare for the assessment and stay certified.
  • An assessment which checks your knowledge on the latest features. 

Please Note:

1. Stay Certified is accessible only to those who have been Certified on SAP Emarsys. If you haven't obtained your certification, please so do here: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-emarsys-customer-engangement

2. You will  need S-User subscription to access Stay Certified as well as Certification.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: "Edit" button not available in FIORI App "Make Compan...
entry.content:
Hi

Please refer to following KBA note for the issue you reported, https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2764392

As per the note, you need check your assigned business role, e.g.SAP_BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT. Please Change the role SAP_BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT in the Business Roles app to allow Write access.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: "Edit" button not available in FIORI App "Make Compan...

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Looking at the configuration changes introduced in the following SAP Note, I am concerned about the manual updates specified for Table V_T596I for subapplication T424 and T425.  It only adds wage type /416 [CPP Second Contrib. ER] to box 27A [Employer's CPP2 contributions], but does not remove the association to Box 27 [Employer's CPP contributions].  Anyone else with this concern?

December 9, 2024

3550220

Legal Change

YE24: Form T4, T4A PDF layout and T4 XML update 2024

This leaves the reporting in the CRA Mag Media, causing the value to be 4055.50 rather than 3867.50, the max CPP.
<tot_empr_cpp_amt>4055.50</tot_empr_cpp_amt><tot_empr_cppe_amt>188.00</tot_empr_cppe_amt>

entry.title: Re: T4/T4A, and reconciliation reports for canada payroll
entry.content: This helps, thanks
